example: The garrulous taxi driver talked nonstop during the entire ride.
mnemonic: '개럴러스' → '개(개)+럴(럴럴)+러스' 개처럼 끊임없이 짖는 수다스러운!

example: It would be presumptuous of me to offer advice to such an expert.
mnemonic: pre(미리) + sumpt(잡다, 라틴어 sumere) + uous = 미리 잡아채는 → 주제넘은. sume(잡다): presume, assume, consume

example: Parents strive to inculcate good values in their children from an early age.
mnemonic: in(안에) + culc(밟다, 라틴어 calcare) + ate = 안에 밟아 넣다 → 반복해서 주입하다

example: His sartorial elegance was admired by everyone at the gala.
mnemonic: sartor(재봉사, 라틴어 sartor) + ial = 재봉사의 → 의복에 관한. sartor(재봉): sartorial, sartorius(재봉근=다리를 꼬는 근육)

example: The storyteller beguiled the children with tales of magical adventures.
mnemonic: be(완전히) + guile(속임수) = 완전히 속임수에 빠뜨리다 → 매혹하다. guile(속임수): wile(계략)과 관련

example: The saturnine man rarely smiled and preferred to be alone.
mnemonic: Saturn(토성). 점성술에서 토성의 영향=우울한 기질 → 음침한. 토성(Saturn)=느리고 어두운 행성

example: The detective compiled a dossier on the suspect's activities.
mnemonic: 프랑스어 dossier(등에 라벨이 붙은 서류철), dos(등, 라틴어 dorsum)에서 유래. 등에 라벨이 붙은 서류 → 서류철

example: The professor's recondite lectures on quantum theory confused most students.
mnemonic: re(뒤로) + cond(숨기다, 라틴어 condere) + ite = 깊이 숨겨진 → 심오한, 난해한

example: The impudent student talked back to the teacher in front of the whole class.
mnemonic: im(아닌) + pud(부끄러운, 라틴어 pudere) + ent = 부끄러움이 없는 → 뻔뻔한. pudor(수치심): impudence

example: Calling it a smartphone is a misnomer since most people rarely use it for calls.
mnemonic: mis(잘못된) + nomer(이름, 라틴어 nomen) = 잘못된 이름 → 잘못된 명칭. nom(이름): nominal, nominate

example: To her chagrin, she discovered she had been passed over for the promotion.
mnemonic: 프랑스어 chagrin(슬픔, 걱정). 기대가 빗나간 분함 → 무안함

example: The drug may cause adverse side effects in some patients.
mnemonic: ad(~쪽으로) + verse(돌다) = 반대로 돌다 → 불리한. vers/vert(돌다): reverse, convert, adversary

example: The company rescinded the job offer after discovering falsified documents.
mnemonic: re(뒤로) + scind(자르다, 라틴어 scindere) = 뒤로 잘라내다 → 철회하다. sciss/scind(자르다): scissors

example: The docile puppy followed its owner obediently throughout the park.
mnemonic: doc(가르치다, 라틴어 docere) + ile = 가르치기 쉬운 → 유순한. doc(가르치다): doctor, doctrine, document

example: Please remove all extraneous information that is not relevant to the report.
mnemonic: extra(밖의) + neous = 외부의 → 관계없는. extra(밖): extraordinary, extravagant, extricate

example: Making invidious comparisons between siblings can harm family relationships.
mnemonic: in(향해) + vid(보다) + ious = 시기의 눈으로 보는 → 불쾌한. vid(보다): invidia(라틴어=시기), envious

example: The incandescent light of the sunset painted the sky in shades of gold.
mnemonic: in(안에서) + cand(빛나다, 라틴어 candere) + escent = 안에서 빛나는 → 백열의. cand(빛나다): candle, candid, candidate

example: The villain cast a baleful glare at the hero.
mnemonic: bale(재앙, 고대 영어 bealu) + ful = 재앙으로 가득한 → 해로운, 악의적인

example: The constant noise from the construction site was a source of great vexation.
mnemonic: vex(괴롭히다, 라틴어 vexare) + ation = 괴로운 상태 → 짜증. vex(괴롭히다): vexatious

example: He was completely oblivious to the danger lurking around the corner.
mnemonic: ob(완전히) + liv(잊다, 라틴어 obliviscor) + ious = 완전히 잊은 → 의식하지 못하는

example: The lithe gymnast performed an incredibly complex routine.
mnemonic: 고대 영어 lithe(부드러운, 유순한). 부드럽게 구부러지는 → 유연한

example: The warm afternoon sun made the students somnolent during the lecture.
mnemonic: somn(잠, 라틴어 somnus) + olent = 잠이 드는 → 졸리운. somn(잠): insomnia, somnambulism(몽유병)

example: Advertisers sometimes use subliminal messages to influence consumer behavior.
mnemonic: sub(아래) + limin(문턱, 라틴어 limen) + al = 의식의 문턱 아래의 → 잠재의식의. limin(문턱): eliminate, preliminary

example: The splenetic critic savaged every new production that opened in the city.
mnemonic: splen(비장, 그리스어 splen) + etic = 비장의 → 심술궂은. 고대 의학: 비장이 화와 우울의 원인이라 믿음. spleen(비장, 울분)

example: The profligate spending habits of the government drew public criticism.
mnemonic: pro(앞으로) + flig(때리다, 라틴어 fligere) + ate = 앞으로 내동댕이치는 → 방탕한. flig/flict(때리다): afflict, conflict

example: A neutral party was called to arbitrate the labor dispute.
mnemonic: arbiter(중재인, 라틴어) + ate = 중재인 역할을 하다 → 중재하다. 어근 arbit(판단하다): arbitrary

example: Please keep your comments germane to the topic under discussion.
mnemonic: '저메인' → 'German(독일)+e' 독일처럼 정확하고 적절한!

example: He had the temerity to challenge the professor's theory in front of the class.
mnemonic: temer(무모한, 라틴어 temerarius) + ity = 무모한 상태 → 만용. 라틴어 temere(무분별하게)

example: The guests maintained decorous behavior throughout the formal dinner.
mnemonic: decor(장식, 적절함, 라틴어 decorum) + ous = 적절한 → 예의 바른. decor(적절함): decorum, decoration

example: As a novice driver, she was very cautious on the highway.
mnemonic: nov(새로운, 라틴어 novus) + ice = 새로 온 사람 → 초보자. nov(새로운): novel, innovate, renovate

example: The apocryphal story about the president's childhood has been debunked by historians.
mnemonic: apo(떨어져) + cryph(숨기다, 그리스어 kryptein) + al = 숨겨진 출처의 → 의심스러운. crypt(숨기다): crypt, cryptic, encrypt

example: The scientist investigated the aberrant data in the experiment.
mnemonic: ab(벗어나) + err(방황하다) + ant = 벗어나 방황하는 → 비정상적인. err 어근: error, erratic, aberration

example: Many considered the demolition of the historic church to be an act of sacrilege.
mnemonic: sacri(신성한, 라틴어 sacer) + lege(훔치다, legere) = 신성한 것을 훔치다 → 신성 모독. sacr(신성한): sacred, sacrifice, sacrament

example: She felt ambivalent about moving to a new city.
mnemonic: ambi(양쪽) + val(가치, 힘) + ent = 양쪽에 가치가 있는 → 상반된 감정의. val(가치): value, valid, equivalent

example: The vandals defiled the ancient temple with graffiti.
mnemonic: de(완전히) + file(더럽히다, 고대 프랑스어 fouler=밟다) = 완전히 더럽히다 → 모독하다

example: The traitor faced universal opprobrium from the citizens of his country.
mnemonic: ob(향해) + probr(수치, 라틴어 probrum) + ium = 수치를 향한 → 치욕. 공개적 비난과 불명예

example: The essay was full of hackneyed phrases that lacked originality.
mnemonic: '핵니드' → 'hackney(세놓는 말)' 너무 많이 탄 말처럼 진부한!

example: In mathematics, the symbol x often denotes an unknown variable.
mnemonic: de(완전히) + note(표시하다, 라틴어 notare) = 완전히 표시하다 → 나타내다. not(표시): note, notation, notorious

example: The camaraderie among the team members was evident in their celebrations.
mnemonic: 프랑스어 camarade(동료), 라틴어 camera(방). 같은 방을 쓰는 동료의 우정 → 동지애

example: The irascible old man yelled at children who walked on his lawn.
mnemonic: ir(라틴어 ira=분노) + ascible = 화가 나기 쉬운. ira(분노): irate, ire

example: The procrustean education system forces all students into the same mold.
mnemonic: Procrustes(프로크루스테스, 그리스 신화). 침대에 맞추어 사람을 늘이거나 자른 강도 → 억지로 맞추는

example: She gave an impromptu speech that captivated the entire audience.
mnemonic: im(아닌) + promptu(준비된, 라틴어 promptus) = 준비되지 않은 → 즉흥의. prompt(준비된, 신속한)

example: The taciturn farmer rarely spoke more than a few words at a time.
mnemonic: tacit(조용한) + urn = 항상 조용한 → 과묵한. tacere(조용히 하다): tacit, reticent

example: He tends to embellish his stories to make them more exciting.
mnemonic: '임벨리시' → 'em+bell(아름다운)+ish' 아름답게 꾸미다!

example: The professor's eccentricity made him both beloved and feared by students.
mnemonic: ec(밖으로) + centr(중심) + icity = 중심에서 벗어남 → 기이함. centr(중심): center, concentrate, egocentric

example: The meeting was desultory, jumping from topic to topic without resolution.
mnemonic: de(아래로) + sult(뛰다, 라틴어 saltare) + ory = 여기저기 뛰어다니는 → 산만한. salt(뛰다): assault, result, insult

example: The story about the politician turned out to be nothing but a canard.
mnemonic: 프랑스어 canard(오리). 반쯤 오리를 팔다(vendre un canard à moitié)=사기치다 → 유언비어

example: The corpulent man struggled to fit into the airplane seat.
mnemonic: corpul(몸, 라틴어 corpus) + ent = 몸이 큰 → 뚱뚱한. corp(몸): corpse, corporation, incorporate

example: The rapacious corporation exploited natural resources without regard for the environment.
mnemonic: rap(잡다, 라틴어 rapere) + acious = 잡아채는 → 탐욕스러운. rap(잡다): raptor, ravish, ravage

example: Growing up in a bilingual household gave her a natural advantage.
mnemonic: bi(둘) + lingu(언어, 라틴어 lingua) + al = 두 언어의. lingu(언어): language, linguist, linguistics
